zh.Frostenden Suffolk England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可福洛斯登
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Martin於1540年出生在英國肯特希布恩時，他的父親William Martin16歲，母親Alice Hylton10歲。他於1565年6月3日與Margaret Collyn在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1630年在英國享年90歲。

zh-hans.Frostenden Suffolk England: 英国英格兰素福爾可福洛斯登
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Martin于1540年出生在英国肯特希布恩时，他的父亲William Martin16岁，母亲Alice Hylton10岁。他于1565年6月3日与Margaret Collyn在英国结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1630年在英国享年90岁。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When William Martin was born in 1540, in Shipbourne, Kent, England, his father, William Martin, was 16 and his mother, Alice Hylton, was 10. He married Margaret Collyn on 3 June 1565, in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1630, in England, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Martin est né en 1540, à Shipbourne, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William Martin, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Alice Hylton, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Collyn le 3 juin 1565, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1630, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Martin 1540 in Shipbourne, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Martin 16 und seine Mutter Alice Hylton 10. Er hat am 3. Juni 1565 in England Margaret Collyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1630 im Alter von 90 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Martin è nato il nel 1540 nel Shipbourne, Kent, England, il suo padre, William Martin, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Alice Hylton, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Collyn il 3 giugno 1565, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1630, nel England, all'età di 90 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Martinが1540年にイギリスケントシュイプボウルンで生まれた時，父William Martinは16歳，母Alice Hyltonは10歳であった。1565年6月3日にイギリスでMargaret Collynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1630年にイギリスで90歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Martin(이)가 1540년 영국 켄트 시보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Martin(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Hylton(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1565년 6월 3일 영국에서 Margaret Collyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1630년 영국에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Martin nasceu em 1540, em Shipbourne, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Martin, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Alice Hylton, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Collyn em 3 de junho de 1565, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1630, em Inglaterra, com 90 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Martin родился в 1540 г. в местности шипбурн, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени William Martin было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Hylton, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Collyn 3 июня 1565 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1630 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 90 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Martin nació en 1540, en Shipbourne, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, William Martin, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Alice Hylton, tenía 10 años. Se casó con Margaret Collyn el 3 de junio de 1565, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1630, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 90 años.
